nativeRange.habitat: Rocky shores and coral reefs; also occurs in estuaries and silty inner reefs at depths of roughly 1–25 m.
nativeRange.biotope: Reef-associated, non-migratory; typically encountered singly or in pairs.

diet.notes: Generally accepts a variety of meaty foods once settled — mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, finely chopped seafood, and frozen carnivore preparations are commonly used. New arrivals are often shy and deliberate feeders; live foods (live brine, live blackworms) are commonly required to start them eating. Renowned for picking at Aiptasia (glass anemones).

summary: Elegant Indo-Pacific butterflyfish prized for its copper-banded body and long snout, often used to control Aiptasia. Generally considered an advanced fish due to feeding challenges.
careNotes: Best housed in a mature, well-established reef or FOWLR aquarium (commonly advised at six months or older) with peaceful tankmates and ample live rock providing crevices for grazing. Generally kept singly unless a confirmed mated pair is acquired together; conspecifics and similar butterflyfishes are usually quarrelsome. Reef compatibility is rated 'with caution' — has been reported to pick at feather dusters, tube worms, clams, ornamental shrimp, and occasionally LPS polyps, while frequently consuming Aiptasia. Feeding the new arrival is the chief husbandry hurdle; many specimens refuse prepared foods initially and require live brine or live blackworms to be coaxed onto frozen mysis and chopped seafood. Tank-size sources spread widely (75–130 US gal / 284–492 L); 400 L (~105 US gal) was chosen on the cautious side to give an active, deliberate hunter swimming room and a long footprint. Most sources advise water temperatures around 24–27 °C; LiveAquaria's range (72–78 °F / 22–26 °C) skews cooler than FishBase's wild data (~24.7–29.3 °C, mean 28.5 °C), so the recorded range averages across both. Reported aquarium lifespan varies (~4 years on the low end to 6–10 years with excellent care).
breedingNotes: Has rarely if ever been bred in home aquaria. Forms monogamous pair bonds in the wild; pair-bonded individuals should not be separated.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (family Chaetodontidae, order Acanthuriformes), max length (20 cm TL), wild temperature range (24.7–29.3 °C), and Western Pacific distribution.

additional.name: Aquarium Source
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank-size guidance (sources spread 75–130 US gal). Reports captive lifespan ~6–10 years and recommends introduction only to tanks established 6+ months.
